summary: Alfred shows healthy refactoring momentum with recent module extractions (handlers, input, colon_commands) addressing bloaters and long methods. However, three critical legacy modules remain: bridge.rs (5669 lines, 70+ register functions), app.rs (6882 lines with 60% test code), and editor_state.rs (2652 lines, 35+ public fields). Bridge duplication patterns are well-understood and could be resolved with generic extractors. EditorState requires strategic decomposition into concern-specific substates. Recent refactorings follow a functional-core pattern that should be extended.

refactoring_priorities.title: Extract argument-extraction helpers and auto-register macros

current_state.issue: Duplicated argument-extraction pattern (~20 occurrences) across register_set_line_style, register_set_line_background, register_define_panel, register_set_panel_line, etc. Each register function follows identical error-handling for int/bool/string args. The pattern repeats match args.get(N) -> match Value::Type -> return error chains.

proposed_solution.approach: Create generic extractors: extract_int_arg, extract_bool_arg, extract_int_arg_at(N) that handle error messages uniformly. Then use a registration macro or factory function to reduce boilerplate.
proposed_solution.example_scope: At least 20 register functions benefit. register_set_line_style, register_set_line_background, register_define_panel, register_set_panel_line, register_set_panel_size, register_set_panel_priority, register_set_panel_style.
proposed_solution.estimated_reduction: ~400 lines of duplicated error-handling boilerplate could be eliminated with generic helpers.

refactoring_priorities.test_impact: Low—test coverage exists, helpers can be property-tested independently

refactoring_priorities.title: Break bridge.rs into functional concerns (buffer, cursor, theme, panel, hook primitives modules)

current_state.issue: Single file covering 70+ distinct register_* functions spanning buffer, cursor, theme, hooks, keymaps, panels, strings, lists, filesystem, and focus primitives. All Lisp–Rust bridging lives in one file. The file is 5.6k lines, making it hard to navigate, test, and reason about concerns.

proposed_solution.approach:
Extract modules by domain:
  - bridge/buffer.rs (register_buffer_insert, register_buffer_delete, register_buffer_content, register_buffer_set_content, register_buffer_line_count, register_buffer_get_line)
  - bridge/cursor.rs (register_cursor_position, register_cursor_move, register_message)
  - bridge/theme.rs (register_set_theme_color, register_get_theme_color, register_define_theme, register_load_theme)
  - bridge/panel.rs (register_define_panel, register_remove_panel, register_set_panel_*, register_set_panel_size, register_set_panel_priority, register_set_panel_style, register_set_panel_content, register_set_panel_line)
  - bridge/hook.rs (register_add_hook, register_dispatch_hook, register_remove_hook)
  - bridge/keymap.rs (register_make_keymap, register_define_key, register_set_active_keymap)
  - bridge/command.rs (register_define_command)
  - bridge/mod.rs re-exports all public register_* functions and calls them from register_all() or similar.
proposed_solution.module_structure:
crates/alfred-lisp/src/bridge/mod.rs (40 lines)
  ├── buffer.rs (~500 lines)
  ├── cursor.rs (~300 lines)
  ├── theme.rs (~600 lines)
  ├── panel.rs (~800 lines)
  ├── hook.rs (~500 lines)
  ├── keymap.rs (~300 lines)
  ├── command.rs (~200 lines)
  └── helpers.rs (~200 lines for arg extraction)
proposed_solution.estimated_reduction: ~500 lines from mod.rs become organization & imports. Readability improves 10x. Each module becomes focused, testable.

refactoring_priorities.test_impact: Medium—no functional change but all 70+ primitives must continue passing tests

refactoring_priorities.title: Decompose EditorState into substates: InputState, NavigationState, SelectionState, ClipboardState, MacroState, SearchState

current_state.issue: EditorState carries 35+ public fields covering buffer, cursor, viewport, registers, undo/redo, macro system, jump list, change list, theme, named_themes, panels, line styles, line backgrounds, keymaps, hooks, and focus state. Single Responsibility is strained. Adding any new feature (marks, registers, search) forces changes to this struct.
current_state.smell_category: Bloaters (Large Struct / God Object) + Change Preventers (Divergent Change)

proposed_solution.approach:
Extract coherent substates and nest them within EditorState:

1. InputState substruct: macro_recording, macro_replaying, macro_buffer, macro_registers, last_macro_register, pending_register
2. NavigationState substruct: jump_list, jump_index, change_list, change_list_index, last_char_find, search_pattern, search_forward, last_edit_command
3. SelectionState substruct: selection_start, visual_line_mode, marks (for vim marks)
4. ClipboardState substruct: registers, yank_linewise (consolidate register metadata)
5. RenderingState substruct: line_styles, line_backgrounds, theme, named_themes, cursor_shapes

Keep in EditorState: buffer, cursor, viewport, commands, keymaps, active_keymaps, hooks, mode, message, running, panels, cli_argument, focused_panel, tab_width.
proposed_solution.new_structure:
pub struct EditorState {
  pub buffer: Buffer,
  pub cursor: Cursor,
  pub viewport: Viewport,
  pub commands: CommandRegistry,
  pub mode: String,
  pub keymaps: HashMap<String, Keymap>,
  pub active_keymaps: Vec<String>,
  pub hooks: HookRegistry,
  pub message: Option<String>,
  pub running: bool,
  pub panels: PanelRegistry,
  pub cli_argument: Option<String>,
  pub focused_panel: Option<String>,
  pub tab_width: usize,
  pub undo_stack: Vec<UndoSnapshot>,  // keep top-level for now
  pub redo_stack: Vec<UndoSnapshot>,  // keep top-level for now
  
  // Nested substates
  pub input: InputState,
  pub navigation: NavigationState,
  pub selection: SelectionState,
  pub clipboard: ClipboardState,
  pub rendering: RenderingState,
}

pub struct InputState {
  pub macro_recording: Option<char>,
  pub macro_replaying: bool,
  pub macro_buffer: Vec<KeyEvent>,
  pub macro_registers: HashMap<char, Vec<KeyEvent>>,
  pub last_macro_register: Option<char>,
  pub pending_register: Option<char>,
}

pub struct NavigationState {
  pub jump_list: Vec<Cursor>,
  pub jump_index: usize,
  pub change_list: Vec<Cursor>,
  pub change_list_index: usize,
  pub last_char_find: Option<(CharFindKind, char)>,
  pub search_pattern: Option<String>,
  pub search_forward: bool,
  pub last_edit_command: Option<String>,
}

pub struct SelectionState {
  pub selection_start: Option<Cursor>,
  pub visual_line_mode: bool,
  pub marks: HashMap<char, Cursor>,
}

pub struct ClipboardState {
  pub registers: HashMap<char, RegisterEntry>,
}

pub struct RenderingState {
  pub line_styles: HashMap<usize, Vec<(usize, usize, ThemeColor)>>,
  pub line_backgrounds: HashMap<usize, (ThemeColor, ThemeColor)>,
  pub theme: Theme,
  pub named_themes: HashMap<String, Theme>,
  pub cursor_shapes: HashMap<String, String>,
}
proposed_solution.migration_approach: Phase 1: Create substates as new types. Phase 2: Add fields to EditorState. Phase 3: Migrate one concern at a time (e.g., all InputState access) with tests passing after each phase. Phase 4: Update handlers module to use substate accessors (e.g., state.input.macro_recording instead of state.macro_recording).
proposed_solution.estimated_reduction: EditorState shrinks from 35 fields to ~15 top-level fields. Each substate becomes a focused, testable unit. Easier to add new features without affecting unrelated state.

dependencies: Unit tests fully passing before refactoring
refactoring_priorities.test_impact: High—every handler that accesses state must be updated to use substate accessors. All ~200 lines of handler code will need updates, but changes are mechanical (state.field -> state.substate.field).
refactoring_priorities.mitigation: Implement getters and setters for backward compatibility. Use feature flags or feature branches to migrate incrementally. Test each phase thoroughly.

refactoring_priorities.title: Extract event-loop dispatcher and command handlers from run() method

current_state.issue: Though 60% is test code, the production run() function contains a long event loop with SaveBuffer, OpenFile, ExecCommand, Eval, and SaveAndQuit arms that could be extracted to a dispatcher module. The event loop borrows state_rc multiple times for change-detection rather than a single snapshot.

proposed_solution.approach:
Create crates/alfred-tui/src/dispatcher.rs with:

1. EventDispatcher struct holding the state_rc and runtime
2. dispatch_deferred_action(action: DeferredAction) -> io::Result<()> method handling SaveBuffer, OpenFile, ExecCommand, Eval, SaveAndQuit
3. Simplify run() to:
   - Initialize dispatcher
   - Loop: read terminal event -> convert -> handle_key_event -> dispatch deferred action
   - Check for state changes and render

Benefit: run() becomes ~200 lines instead of 900+. Dispatcher can be tested independently.
proposed_solution.estimated_reduction: ~300 lines from app.rs, readability improved 5x, testability enabled

refactoring_priorities.test_impact: Medium—dispatcher logic can be tested with fixtures, integration tests remain in app tests

refactoring_priorities.title: Extract match branches from handle_key_event into InputState strategy handlers

current_state.issue: handle_key_event() spans ~400 lines with 12 distinct if-let / match branches (one per InputState variant), each containing non-trivial logic. The function handles macro recording, command mode, search mode, PendingChar, PendingReplace, PendingMark, PendingJumpMark, PendingRegister, PendingMacroRecord, PendingMacroPlay, OperatorPending, and TextObject states.

proposed_solution.estimated_reduction: ~200 lines from input.rs main function, each strategy becomes focused, easier to test
proposed_solution.impl_structure:
crates/alfred-tui/src/input/
  ├── mod.rs (the main handle_key_event dispatcher, ~100 lines)
  ├── strategies.rs or separate files for each state
  ├── normal.rs (NormalStateHandler, ~150 lines)
  ├── command.rs (CommandStateHandler, ~100 lines)
  ├── search.rs (SearchStateHandler, ~80 lines)
  ├── pending.rs (PendingCharHandler, PendingReplaceHandler, etc., ~300 lines)
  └── operator.rs (OperatorPendingHandler, TextObjectHandler, ~150 lines)

refactoring_priorities.test_impact: High—each strategy can be tested independently with focused test cases

refactoring_priorities.title: Fix Feature Envy: replace multiple state borrows with single snapshot for change detection

current_state.issue: The run() event loop directly accesses state_rc.borrow().buffer.version(), state_rc.borrow().cursor, state_rc.borrow().mode, and state_rc.borrow().message across multiple borrows for change-detection, rather than a single snapshot or dedicated change-set return value from handle_key_event.

proposed_solution.benefit: Single borrow of state, clearer rendering decisions, easier to extend with new state fields

refactoring_priorities.test_impact: Low—change detection is testable in isolation

current_state.issue: rainbow_csv.rs exists as a file in alfred-core/src but is not exported from lib.rs (not listed in pub mod declarations). The module is orphaned from the crate's public API.

proposed_solution.approach: Delete crates/alfred-core/src/rainbow_csv.rs. If functionality is needed, implement as a pure Lisp plugin (plugins/rainbow-csv/init.lisp) instead, matching the browser and other plugins.

refactoring_priorities.title: Extract magic numbers into named constants

current_state.issue: Hardcoded poll timeout of 100ms (Duration::from_millis(100)) and fallback terminal size (80, 24) are inline literals with no named constant.

phase_3_architectural.risk_mitigation: Use feature flags and branch-based development; extensive testing after each phase

single_responsibility.findings: bridge.rs violates SRP by registering 70+ distinct primitives; should be split by domain. EditorState accumulates too many concerns (editing, navigation, selection, clipboard, rendering, input). Most handlers and modules (buffer, cursor, editor_state) are well-focused.

open_closed.findings: Well-designed overall. New features typically extend via Lisp plugins or new handlers without modifying core logic. handler module uses data-driven registration. Bridge primitives are extensible but currently sprawl across one file.
recommendations: Refactor bridge to make adding primitives easier (auto-registration macros)

liskov_substitution.findings: No widespread violations. Handler trait and hook system are correctly substitutable.

interface_segregation.findings: EditorState exposes 35+ fields; clients often need only a subset (e.g., input module accesses 15 fields). Creating substates improves this. Bridge also exports a large API without clear grouping.

dependency_inversion.findings: Core logic (handlers, buffer, cursor) depends on abstraction (CommandRegistry, HookRegistry). Good. But input.rs depends directly on EditorState fields (tight coupling). Lisp bridge captures Rc<RefCell<EditorState>> in closures, creating coupling.
recommendations: Introduce facade or substate accessors to reduce input.rs coupling

severity_breakdown.critical: bridge.rs size (5669 lines) makes navigation and feature addition slow. Duplication pattern is well-understood and fixable.
severity_breakdown.high: EditorState God Object tendency forces changes when adding new features. Requires strategic decomposition.
severity_breakdown.medium: app.rs long event loop and input.rs long match statement reduce readability. Both have clear extraction paths.
severity_breakdown.low: Magic numbers, inconsistent naming (register_string_primitives vs define_native_closure), dead code

payoff_areas: Faster feature development (new primitives, new handlers)
Reduced bug-fix time (smaller, focused modules are easier to test)
Improved onboarding (new developers can understand one concern at a time)
Easier testing (extracted modules can be unit-tested independently)

recent_refactoring_trajectory.observation: Excellent momentum. Last 6 commits show deliberate extraction patterns: handlers module (commit 343b614), input.rs (commit 28aea08), colon_commands.rs (commit ea4f19e), facade migration for buffer/viewport/cursor/mode (commits 8e99b73, 0ec6091, 8bb828c). Team is actively reducing large modules.
recent_refactoring_trajectory.pattern: Functional-core / imperative-shell architecture is being reinforced through facade and strategy extraction.
recent_refactoring_trajectory.next_logical_step: Continue the momentum by addressing the two largest remaining modules: bridge.rs (extract by domain) and app.rs (extract dispatcher). EditorState decomposition is the strategic, higher-risk follow-up.

codebase_health.reasoning: Strong foundational patterns (functional core, data-driven handlers, plugin system). Identified smells are well-documented and have clear refactoring paths. Recent trajectory shows deliberate movement toward smaller, focused modules. Main risk is that bridge.rs and EditorState will become harder to work with as more features are added.
codebase_health.trend: Positive. The codebase is being actively refactored toward better structure.
